channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario budget to be tabled at Queen’s Park. Here is what we already know will be in it
Premier Doug Ford, health minister defend supervised consumption site closures
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford and his health minister said Wednesday they will not reverse course on their decision to shutter more supervised drug consumption sites, after a letter from six former Toronto mayors criticized the move to defund the sites.

item.title: Ontario education minister tells schools to keep graduation ceremonies strictly ‘apolitical’
item.description: Ontario’s education minister defended a letter he sent to school boards this week, calling for graduation ceremonies to be 'strictly student-centred, apolitical, inclusive and respectful.'

item.title: Ontario health minister 'disappointed' in feds' approach to nurse practitioner rules
item.description: Ontario’s health minister said Wednesday she is 'disappointed' the federal government has left provinces and territories on their own to set rules for publicly funding nurse practitioners instead of creating a consistent, national standard.

item.title: All Ontario buyers will be eligible for HST rebate on new homes for one year, Ford says
item.description: Premier Doug Ford says the province and federal government plan to temporarily expand the HST rebate for new homes in the province, allowing all buyers a rebate of up to $130,000.

item.title: Ontarians oppose Ford government freedom of information changes, poll finds
item.description: A poll commissioned by Ontario’s largest public sector union shows widespread disapproval of the Ford government’s recent changes to freedom-of-information (FOI) laws.

item.title: Doug Ford says privacy commissioner opposing record restrictions ‘politically driven’
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Tuesday he believes the information and privacy commissioner, who is speaking out about changes he is making to freedom-of-information laws, is 'politically driven.'

item.title: Ford says to ‘stay tuned’ on HST changes for home-buyers
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says his government will unveil details Wednesday about potential changes around HST rules for homebuyers.

item.title: Students protest OSAP changes at Queen’s Park
item.description: Students and union members are expected to descend on Queen’s Park today for another large protest over the government’s changes to student assistance.

item.title: Ontario to seize ownership of Toronto Island Airport lands and declare it a special economic zone
item.description: Premier Doug Ford says the provincial government will be seizing ownership of city-owned lands at Toronto’s Billy Bishop Airport and declaring it a special economic zone, invoking new powers that will allow it to override environmental and other regulations.

item.title: Speaker says NDP motion can’t result in warrant for Ford’s phone records
item.description: MPPs are returning to Queen’s Park for the first time since rising for a break from the legislature before Christmas. Follow along here for live updates:

item.title: Opposition parties to pressure Ford government to reverse OSAP changes as legislature resumes
item.description: Opposition parties plan to lean on the Ford government to backtrack on contentious changes to student aid as MPPs return to Queen’s Park for the first time since December.

item.title: MPPs return to Queen’s Park for the first time in 102 days. Here’s what you can expect
item.description: MPPs are returning to the legislature at Queen’s Park this week for the first time since rising for a winter break from the chamber in December. Here’s some of what to expect in the next few weeks.

item.title: Queen's Park resumes sitting after 14-week break, straight into budget season
item.description: Ontario’s legislature is set to resume sitting Monday after a 14-week break that ended in a veritable deluge of news, partial proposals and headline-grabbing musings from Premier Doug Ford and his government.

item.title: Quebec premier dismisses Doug Ford’s concerns over province’s EV targets
item.description: Quebec Premier François Legault is dismissing his Ontario counterpart’s concerns over Quebec’s electric vehicle mandate.
